response.justification: The candidate is an experienced, versatile senior full-stack developer with strong craftsmanship credentials (TDD, testing tooling, community teaching), but the profile is a poor match for this role's core requirements. The position demands 8 years of C++ and deep cloud compute platform experience; the resume shows only a brief C/C++ stint (~6 months at National Instruments) with the bulk of the career in TypeScript/Node.js, DLang, and web/product work. High-scale exposure exists (Sociomantic's low-latency data integration handling millions of requests/sec) but is limited to roughly two years and was in DLang, not C++. There is also no formal technical-leadership or cloud/DevOps mentoring role on record.
strengths: Strong software craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, testing frameworks, and active knowledge-sharing through workshops and talks
Real high-scale, low-latency systems experience at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic (millions of HTTP requests per second)
Broad polyglot experience across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded, plus Kubernetes/Docker deployment of his own platform
concerns: Falls far short of the 8-year C++ requirement — only a brief professional C/C++ engagement
Limited large-scale infrastructure/distributed-systems depth relative to the 5-year requirement, and none in cloud compute platforms specifically
No demonstrated formal technical-leadership role managing priorities, deadlines, and cross-team alignment

key_factors.factor: Mentoring and engineering best-practices track record
